Code fragment: public final TableField<MessagesRecord, Integer> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is generically named 'id' instead of a more descriptive name like 'message_id'. Consider renaming the column to 'message_id' to make it self-documenting and avoid ambiguity in queries with joins.

Code fragment: public final TableField<UsersRecord, Integer> ID = createField(DSL.name("id"), SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key is named just 'id' which is a generic, non-descriptive name that doesn't convey the table context. Consider renaming it to 'user_id' to make it more meaningful and to avoid ambiguity in queries with joins.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the MESSAGES table, which can retrieve unnecessary data and hurt performance. Replace with `select(Messages.MESSAGES.ID, Messages.MESSAGES.USER_ID, Messages.MESSAGES.CONTENT, ...)` to explicitly list only the required columns.

Using `select()` without specifying columns fetches all columns from the Users table, which is inefficient and can cause issues if the table schema changes. Replace `select()` with explicit column selection like `select(Users.USERS.ID, Users.USERS.USERNAME, Users.USERS.PASSWORD, Users.USERS.ROLE)`.
